blurb:
Junior's family suddenly developed allergies to him after years together. That was their story, anyway. So they dropped him at the county shelter, and he spent months watching people walk past his kennel. Black dog syndrome is real, and Junior felt it.

Here's what makes this frustrating: Junior is an absolute gem in a home. He's house trained, well-mannered, and incredibly loving. His favorite volunteer takes him out for ice cream at Bruster's and Pelican's, and then he goes to her house sometimes. He does awesome there. He knows how to be a family dog because he was one for years.

Junior prefers to be the only dog and does best with older kids, but if you're looking for a loyal companion who's going to appreciate every single day with you, this is your guy. He's a black Lab who's already proven he's the most devoted friend you could ask for. He just needs someone who won't give up on him when it's inconvenient.

Our Pals Place is in Marietta. You can find their contact information and adoption process on their website or Petfinder page.

Luke's story starts with his mom, Miss Daisy, a gorgeous shepherd who lost her person unexpectedly. The boyfriend didn't want to deal with Daisy and her two puppies, so he dropped them all at the county shelter. No family member stepped up. The shelter held them for weeks hoping someone would come forward, then finally reached out to rescues for help.

Once Luke got to Our Pals Place, he could finally be a puppy again. He got to go outside and play, and lucky for him, he arrived during their youth summer camp, so he had kids walking him on trails and giving him attention all day. Luke is a fun-loving, playful boy who's on the smaller side for a shepherd. His face looks more like a Belgian Malinois while his mom looks like a German Shepherd.

He's a loving young dog looking for a family that won't abandon him when life gets complicated. Luke's been through enough upheaval for one lifetime. He's ready to settle in somewhere permanent and just be the good dog he clearly is.

Our Pals Place is in Marietta. You can find their contact information and adoption process on their website or Petfinder page.
scoring_notes:
• Multi-generational abandonment story creates powerful emotional pull
• Young shepherd with camp experience appeals to active families
• Smaller size makes him accessible to more potential adopters

blurb:
Tiny lives up to her name. She's small and delicate, but she's got the curiosity of a cat twice her size. She came to Good Mews from a county shelter and has been busy exploring every corner and investigating every toy she can find. She's got this gentle, playful energy that makes everything she does look effortless.

She's the perfect mix of active and affectionate. She'll chase things around when the mood strikes, then settle in somewhere cozy when she's done. If you're looking for a cat who's engaging but not overwhelming, she's got that balance figured out.

Tiny is ready to find someone who'll appreciate her small stature and big personality. Good Mews is a cage-free shelter on Robinson Road in Marietta. Submit an inquiry online or call to set up a visit.
scoring_notes:
• Perfect balance of active and affectionate appeals to broad audience
• Small size and gentle nature ideal for many living situations
• Straightforward adoption case with no behavioral challenges

blurb:
His name is Pork Tenderloin and he knows exactly what he wants. This handsome guy likes attention, but only when he's in the mood for it. He'll come find you when he wants pets, not the other way around. Some people might call that attitude, but it's really just good boundaries.

Pork Tenderloin can get overwhelmed faster than other cats, so he does best in a calm house with someone who gets how cats work. He's not going to be everyone's cup of tea, but for the right person who respects his space and lets him call the shots, he's going to be incredibly special.

He came from a county shelter and landed at Good Mews for a better shot at finding his match. If you're already a cat person who knows how to read body language and doesn't need a lap cat, this might be your guy. Good Mews is on Robinson Road in Marietta. Call or submit an inquiry to meet him.
scoring_notes:
• Unique name will grab reader attention and generate discussion
• Honest portrayal helps find the right match rather than returns
• Appeals to experienced cat owners who understand selective personalities

blurb:
Beignet came back to Good Mews through no fault of her own. She was adopted from them as a kitten years ago, but somehow ended up scared and alone at a county shelter. The details don't matter now. What matters is she's safe again and ready for round two.

She spent some time with a foster family to rebuild her confidence, and it worked. She loves being petted and is a social eater who likes company during meals. When she wants attention, she'll sit close or lean against you. She can give gentle love bites when she gets overstimulated, but that just means she needs someone who understands cat language.

Beignet deserves a home where she'll be loved and never have to worry again. She's been through enough. Good Mews is a no-kill, cage-free shelter on Robinson Road in Marietta. Submit an inquiry online or call to start the adoption process.
scoring_notes:
• Heartbreaking return story that readers will connect with emotionally
• Already rebuilt confidence through fostering showing clear progress
• Second chance narrative creates urgency for permanent loving home

blurb:
Otis was on a kill list at the county shelter, not because he'd done anything wrong, but just because his number came up. When the rescue team found him, he was so stressed in the overcrowded, loud environment that he was urinating on himself. The second they got him into a quiet room, he immediately buried his head in someone's lap and calmed right down. He just needed out of there.

At Our Pals Place, he's completely decompressed into the dog he actually is. Fun-loving, attention-seeking, eager to please. He remembered his house training right away, sits politely, and his tail never stops wagging. All the good dog behavior that stress had knocked right out of him came flooding back once he felt safe again.

Whoever adopts Otis is getting a loyal, loving companion who's already proven he can bounce back from the worst circumstances. He's an adult mixed breed, so no surprises about size or temperament. Just a solid, grateful dog who knows what it's like to almost lose everything and won't take a good home for granted.

Our Pals Place is in Marietta. You can find their contact information and adoption process on their website or Petfinder page.
scoring_notes:
• Dramatic rescue from kill list creates compelling urgency
• Complete behavioral transformation shows his true loving nature
• House-trained adult with proven resilience appeals to practical adopters
